sourceMovie.title: We Bury the Dead
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit We Bury the Dead for the same reason it broke through in 2026: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Zak Hilditch keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: World War Z
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If We Bury the Dead clicked for you, World War Z (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after We Bury the Dead?
faq.answer: Try World War Z, 28 Days Later, 28 Weeks Later—each shares DNA with We Bury the Dead while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is We Bury the Dead?
faq.answer: We Bury the Dead (2026) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is We Bury the Dead worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
